title: PM Issue Disposition Semantic Hardening
objective: Harden the existing Class C issue disposition seam so PM resolve_escalated, re_escalate, and return_to_lead actions require explicit source status, target status, actor project scope, and PM reason without adding any endpoint, service, schema, auth, ingress, or autonomous AI mutation authority.
required_inputs: PM Lane 009 read-only live workfront smoke extension
PM Lane 003 return_to_lead semantic hardening precedent
Read-only hosted smoke attempt showing mutation-seam schedule/projects HTTP 500 and pm-workfront HTTP 404 on the public host
constrained_outputs: Shared PM issue-disposition validator now covers return_to_lead, resolve_escalated, and re_escalate
resolve_escalated requires an escalated source issue, payload.status=resolved, actor project scope, and non-empty PM reason
re_escalate requires an in_review source issue, payload.status=escalated, actor project scope, and non-empty PM reason
return_to_lead preserves the existing escalated source issue, payload.status=in_review, actor project scope, and non-empty PM reason contract
Existing approval and static demo callers now send explicit status or reason where the hardened seam requires it

No new endpoint, SQL, schema migration, live database write, service admission, auth or ingress widening, assignment mutation, schedule mutation, Operations Visibility reopening, or autonomous AI business-state action
validation_steps: Run focused mutation-seam PM issue disposition, pipeline integration, and PM workfront read-model tests under SEAM_STORE_BACKEND=memory
Run py_compile for the changed mutation-seam service and validation harness modules
Run focused PM workfront Playwright smoke
Run packet JSON validation and git diff whitespace checks before publication
Record that hosted PM live-data proof remains blocked by stale or failing public deployment state and was not claimed as passing
sql_permission_note: Packet 2026-05-15-pm-lane-010 changes only existing mutation-seam issue disposition semantics, tests, validation harness calls, and existing operations-web callers. No SQL, schema migration, production data write, auth, ingress, service, or new mutation endpoint is authorized.
